seoTitle: How to Create and Start a New Sprint in Jira
metaDescription: Learn how to create a new sprint in Jira, add issues from the backlog, set sprint dates and a goal, and start the sprint.
intro: Getting a new sprint set up correctly from the start makes the rest of the cycle run more smoothly. This guide covers creating a sprint in Jira, pulling in issues from the backlog, setting dates and a goal, and starting it.

steps.heading: Open the Scrum backlog
steps.body: Navigate to your project's backlog view where sprints are created and managed.

steps.heading: Create a new sprint
steps.body: Use the create sprint option to add a new sprint container above your backlog items.

steps.heading: Add issues and set a sprint goal
steps.body: Drag issues from the backlog into the new sprint, and write a short goal describing what the sprint should accomplish.

steps.heading: Start the sprint
steps.body: Set the sprint's start and end dates, then click Start to move the sprint into active status.

faq.question: How do I create a new sprint in Jira?
faq.answer: Open the backlog view and use the create sprint option to add a new sprint container above your backlog items.

faq.question: How do I add issues to a newly created sprint?
faq.answer: Drag issues from the backlog directly into the sprint container, or use the issue's Sprint field to assign it.

faq.question: Do I need to set a sprint goal before starting it?
faq.answer: It's best practice to set a sprint goal, though Jira technically allows you to start a sprint without one.

faq.question: Can I change the sprint dates after starting it?
faq.answer: Sprint dates are typically set before starting, though some adjustments may still be possible depending on your workflow settings.
